strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 adds a dedicated `parseVersionFormat` function alongside the pre-existing `parseNameFlag` helper, mirroring the codebase's established per-flag-parser pattern, whereas P1 inlines the `--format` parsing (indexOf/lookup/validation) directly inside the `case 'version':` block, breaking from that convention.

P1's error path distinguishes a missing `--format` value from an invalid one via `format || '(missing)'` in the error message, while P2's `parseVersionFormat` emits the same generic `--format only supports json` message regardless of whether the value was wrong or absent, giving strictly less diagnostic coverage for that edge case.

P1's new JSON test asserts `assert.match(parsed.version, /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/)`, staying version-agnostic like the pre-existing `version prints package version` test, whereas P2's test hardcodes `assert.strictEqual(out, '{"version":"0.1.0"}\n')`, which will spuriously fail the very next time `package.json`'s version is bumped.

P2 collapses the version case body to two clean lines (`const format = parseVersionFormat(rest); const version = readPackageVersion();`) by pushing validation into a named, reusable function, while P1 leaves a 7-line block of inline indexOf/ternary/if-exit logic directly in the switch case, making that call site harder to scan and modify.
